summary: M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 is started after M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 was accepted and integrated through PR #125. The product owner explicitly resolved the manual node battle Human Gate as DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and authorized M5 Exit Gate to begin. This start record does not pass M5, does not create docs/GATE-M5.md, does not start M6, and does not change product/runtime code.

tests_run.result: M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 was the next dependency-ready task before this start checkpoint.

tests_run.command: node .agents/skills/monsoon-studio-orchestrator/scripts/taskctl.mjs set-status M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 CLOSED; taskctl set-status M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 READY; taskctl set-status M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 IN_PROGRESS

M5 Exit lead thread recorded in task registry.

decisions: Start M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 because the manual node battle Human Gate is resolved and integrated.
Do not pass M5 in this start checkpoint.
Do not start M6 in this start checkpoint.
Do not create manual node battle implementation or blocking research/spike work.
risks: M5 Exit must still validate all M5 evidence and receive independent acceptance before any M5_GATE result is integrated.
Formal M5 passage must distinguish PASS, PASS_WITH_LIMITS, BLOCKED, and follow-up routing without hiding known limitations.
requested_action: After this closure/start checkpoint enters main, continue M5 Exit Gate evaluation and spawn required reviewers for the gate evidence.
